# Output format test for mcpspy
#
# This script tests mcpspy's ability to handle different output formats,
# logging options, and file output configurations.

# Setup test files with sample MCP messages
-- simple_message.json --
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"req1","method":"initialize","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":"req2","method":"tools/list","params":{}}

# Test standard output format
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -v -f standard_output.mcp -- echo "Standard format"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output appears in stderr
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "mcp-recv" standard_output.mcp
grep "initialize" standard_output.mcp
stdout "Standard format"

# Test JSON format (output file should be valid JSON)
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -v -f json_output.json -json-format -- echo "JSON format"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output with JSON format
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
grep "\"direction\": \"recv\"" json_output.json
grep "\"content\": {" json_output.json
stdout "JSON format"

# Test timestamp formats
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -v -ts-format "15:04:05" -f timestamp_output.mcp -- echo "Custom timestamp"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output with custom timestamp format
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
# Check for time format HH:MM:SS
grep -E "[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}" timestamp_output.mcp
stdout "Custom timestamp"

# Test without timestamps
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -v -no-timestamps -f no_timestamp_output.mcp -- echo "No timestamps"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output still works without timestamps
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
! grep -E "[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}" no_timestamp_output.mcp
stdout "No timestamps"

# Test file output with append mode
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -v -f append_output.mcp -- echo "First write"
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -v -f append_output.mcp -a -- echo "Append mode"
! stderr "error"
# Verify colored output in append mode
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for recv messages
# Count number of occurrences of "initialize" - should be at least 2 with append
grep -c "initialize" append_output.mcp | grep -v "^1$"
stdout "Append mode"

# Test with verbose output off
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -f quiet_output.mcp -- echo "Quiet mode"
! stderr "error"
# No colored output without -v flag
! stderr "\033\\[32m" # No color codes without verbose mode
# File should still contain the messages
grep "initialize" quiet_output.mcp
grep "tools/list" quiet_output.mcp
stdout "Quiet mode"

# Test with very verbose mode (should show additional details with color)
cat simple_message.json | mcpspy -vv -f veryverbose_output.mcp -- echo "Very verbose mode"
! stderr "error"
# Verify both normal and additional colored output
stderr "\033\\[32m" # Green color code for standard messages
stderr "#>"      # Marker for very verbose raw input
stdout "Very verbose mode"

# Test with non-existent command (should capture error)
! mcpspy -v -f error_output.mcp -- non_existent_command
stderr "executable file not found"
# Output file should still be created
test -f error_output.mcp
